title: Lemma 2.6: well-definedness and injectivity of the map phi (apex tree lemma)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The heart of the apex tree lemma is the claimed INJECTION
  phi : {spanning trees R of G containing apex edge e} -> {2-forests {A,B} with F_{A,B}(x) != 0},
from which |a|^2 = (1/tau) sum F >= tau_e/tau = |v*|^2.

Two subtle points need a full argument:

(a) WELL-DEFINEDNESS / existence of index ell. From the linear relation among cut vectors chi_{G-v,v} = sum_j chi_{R_j, R-R_j} (where R_1,...,R_k are components of R - v) and chi_{G-v,v}.x = a.v != 0, some summand chi_{R_ell,R-R_ell}.x != 0. To make phi deterministic, fix ell (e.g. minimal such index). Also justify chi_{G-v,v}.x = a.v via eq:chi_formula and the identification x_C = image of a.

(b) INJECTIVITY via reconstruction. Given phi(R) = {A,B} (a 2-forest obtained from R by deleting an edge incident to v), recover R: if neither A nor B contains e then R = A ∪ B ∪ e; otherwise (wlog e in A) then v,v_infty in A, B is a subtree of T not containing v, and there is a UNIQUE edge f from v to a vertex of B (since T is a tree), giving R = A ∪ B ∪ f. Each implication ('unique f', 'B avoids v') must be proved, and one must verify the two cases are exhaustive and the reconstruction lands back on a tree containing e.

This is the most error-prone step of the whole proof. Verified numerically on random apex graphs (~2000 trees, inequality |a|^2 >= |v*|^2 never violated).
